name: Birling Gap and Seven Sisters Country Park
content: Seven Sisters Country Park is located near Birling Gap, so I'll file it under Birling Gap. If you're like me and grew up in the central coast region of California, Birling Gap will feel a little reminiscent of Point Reyes. For anyone who grew up in Birling Gap, the relationship is likely reversed. From what I understand, Seven Sisters is conceptually similar to the Cliffs of Dover, in that the main attraction are the massive white chalk cliffs. Generally a fairly pleasant place, and I would recommend going. I will give one warning, however, which is that the only bus service to the national park from Eastbourne (the nearest city) is operated by a tourism company. I recommend checking the bus schedule ahead of time, so that you do not become stranded. Like I did, alongside several other tourists who had walking shoes less comfortable than mine. If you do become stranded by the bus, never fear! The nearest public bus stop, along with the nearest restaurant, is in East Dean, which is only a short hike from Seven Sisters.
